research.interface: Meraki Dashboard API v1 REST (organization networks and devices, Link pagination, shard redirects)

official_sources.title: Authentication - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: v1 authenticates with the standard Authorization header valued 'Bearer <API key>'; v0's X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key header must not be used; API keys are secrets that must never be logged

official_sources.title: How to Use the Cisco Meraki Dashboard API - Cisco Meraki Documentation
official_sources.used_for: api.meraki.com redirects calls to the organization's dashboard shard; a GET redirects with 302 and non-GETs with 307/308; many HTTP clients drop the Authorization header or substitute GET on redirect, so clients must handle redirects deliberately; 429 responses set the Retry-After header

official_sources.title: Pagination - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: perPage/startingAfter/endingBefore parameters and the RFC 5988 Link response header carrying up to first, prev, next, last relations whose URLs should be used directly rather than rebuilt

official_sources.title: Rate Limit - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: 10 requests per second per organization; HTTP 429 with Retry-After seconds and body {"errors": ["API rate limit exceeded for organization"]}

official_sources.title: Get Organization Networks - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations/{organizationId}/networks (perPage 3-100000) returning id, name, productTypes per network

official_sources.title: Get Organization Devices - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices (perPage 3-5000, default 1000) returning serial, name, model, networkId, productType, mac per device

research.language_rationale: The CMDB import tooling this feeds is a PowerShell shop. The Dashboard API is plain REST with bearer auth, Link-header pagination and shard redirects, and Meraki publishes no official PowerShell module, so an idiomatic pwsh module over the documented v1 REST contract (with explicit redirect and Retry-After handling that PowerShell's default web cmdlets get wrong) is the credible integration path.

verified_facts: Dashboard API v1 uses the standard Authorization header with a 'Bearer <key>' value on every request against the /api/v1 base path; the v0 X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key header is obsolete.

api.meraki.com answers API calls with redirects to the organization's own dashboard shard host; GETs redirect with 302, other verbs with 307/308, and clients must ensure the Authorization header survives only legitimate Meraki redirect targets since generic HTTP libraries drop auth or downgrade verbs on redirect.

Regional/government dashboards live on distinct Meraki host families (api.meraki.ca, api.meraki.cn, api.meraki.in, api.gov-meraki.com), which defines the trusted Meraki host suffixes for redirect auth preservation.
Paginated list operations answer with an RFC 5988 Link header holding up to first/prev/next/last relations; the documented pattern is to request the next page with the provided URL as-is.
Exceeding the 10 requests/second/organization budget returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header (seconds) and the standard {"errors": [...]} envelope.

GET /organizations/{organizationId}/networks returns id/name/productTypes per network and GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices returns serial/name/model/networkId/productType per device; both are perPage-paginated collections.
